converted from EAD 1.0 to EAD 2002 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Elmer S. Phillips papers
Phillips, E. S.
21-42-3218
3.2 cubic feet. 3.2 cubic feet.
[ca.1936-1994]. Correspondence, lecture notes, publications, photographs, slides, films, and other papers. Correspondence, lecture notes, publications, photographs, slides, and other papers of a Communications professor. Includes photographs of the 1937 Ithaca flood, aerial views of Cornell (1937) and movie films taken by Phillips, including Cornell University scenes, faculty members, academic activities, sports, campus events and agrciultural activities at Cornell. Includes silent black and white film, silent color film, and sound color film and copies of the American Wine Society Journal and the Tompkins County Hospital newsletter, Hospital Highlights. Collection material in English 2704635
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
Damon Clark
Date completed:
June 15, 2002
Date modified:
Marcie Farwell, February 2015
RELATED MATERIALS
The films Helen Powell Smith and Railway Exhibit - Farm and Home Special from this collection have been digitized and can be found on Cornell's Library Media Space website along with other Home Economics and related films.
Cite As:
Elmer S. Phillips papers, #21-42-3218. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Biographical / Historical
Elmer Strobel Phillips was a Professor Emeritus, Communication, New York State College of Agriculture and Life Sciences at Cornell University. Phillips was a founding member of a wine appreciation society in Ithaca that later became the American wine society.
Scope and content
Correspondence, lecture notes, publications, photographs, slides, and other papers of a Communications professor. Includes photographs of the 1937 Ithaca flood, aerial views of Cornell (1937) and movie films taken by Phillips, including Cornell University scenes, faculty members, academic activities, sports, campus events and agricultural activities at Cornell.
Scope and content
Includes silent black and white film, silent color film, and sound color film of Cornell University campus scenes, faculty members, academic activities, sports, campus events, and agricultural activities at Cornell.
Scope and content
Includes copies of the American Wine Society Journal and the Tompkins County Hospital newsletter, Hospital Highlights.
Motion pictures (visual works). Photographs. Floods -- New York (State) -- Ithaca. Communication -- Study and teaching. American Wine Society Journal. Hospital Highlights. New York State College of Agriculture and Life Sciences -- : Faculty. Cornell University -- : Athletics. Cornell University -- : Buildings. Cornell University -- : Description -- : Views. Cornell University -- : Faculty. Cornell University -- : Students.
